subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the character and psychology of Apoorva
Understand the sequence of events leading to the climax
Identify the narrative techniques and descriptive imagery used by the author
Develop inferential and critical reading skills
Summarize the story with interpretation and evaluation

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Contextual Discussion
topics_covered: Author: Anthony Hope and writing style
Introduction to Apoorva and his character
Psychological aspects of fear and imagination
teaching_strategy: Teacher-led discussion on fear and perception
Board notes on character analysis and story setting
student_activities: Predict story events based on title and introduction
Discuss experiences with fear and imagination

lesson_plan.title: Reading – Character and Initial Conflict
topics_covered: Introduction of mirror and snake imagery
His initial reaction and thought process
teaching_strategy: Guided reading with explanation of difficult phrases
student_activities: Underline passages showing fear and imagination
Write 2–3 sentences analyzing Apoorva’s initial reaction

lesson_plan.title: Rising Action and Psychological Analysis
topics_covered: Events leading to panic and suspense
Imagery and descriptive details enhancing fear
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on the cause-and-effect relationship
student_activities: List the sequence of events causing fear
Explain in own words why Apoorva reacts as he does

lesson_plan.title: Climax, Resolution, and Character Insight
topics_covered: Apoorva realizes the snake is a reflection
Character insight: lessons learned and behavioral change
teaching_strategy: Flowchart of events from rising action to resolution
Discussion on learning from the story
student_activities: Write a paragraph describing the climax
Discuss character changes and lesson learned

lesson_plan.title: Themes, Literary Devices, and Summary
topics_covered: Themes: Fear, imagination, and human psychology
Writing a detailed summary with interpretation
teaching_strategy: Board explanation of themes and devices
Guided discussion for inferential summary writing
student_activities: Identify literary devices used by the author
Write a 6–8 sentence summary including inferred lessons

lesson_plan.title: Vocabulary, Reflection, and Real-Life Application
topics_covered: Important words and expressions from the chapter
Reflective writing on fear management and perception
Applying story insights to personal experiences
teaching_strategy: Board explanation of vocabulary and usage
Discussion prompts for reflection and application
student_activities: List new words with meanings and use them in sentences
Write a reflective paragraph on handling fear and imagination
Class discussion on psychological lessons from the story

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze the psychological aspects of fear, discuss how imagination affects perception, and relate the story’s lessons to their own experiences.
